text: Ultimately what I love about a beginner's mind is that you get to be receptive to what's around you. It allows you to be present without judgment.
author: Yo-Yo Ma

text: Because I think I am making progress.
author: Pablo Casals
source: Casals' reply, at age 93, when asked why he still practiced cello for hours each day; reported in The New York Times, 1946

text: It is a sort of benediction on the house. It fills me with awareness of the wonder of life. Each day is something new, fantastic, unbelievable.
author: Pablo Casals
source: Joys and Sorrows (1970), on his daily ritual of playing Bach each morning

text: I have always regarded technique as a means, not an end in itself. The most perfect technique is that which is not noticed at all.
author: Pablo Casals

text: If you continue to work and to absorb the beauty in the world about you, you find that age does not necessarily mean getting old.
author: Pablo Casals

text: The purpose of art is not the release of a momentary ejection of adrenalin but is, rather, the gradual, lifelong construction of a state of wonder and serenity.
author: Glenn Gould
source: Quoted in Geoffrey Payzant, Glenn Gould: Music and Mind (1978)

text: If you practice slowly, you forget slowly; if you practice fast, you forget fast.
author: Itzhak Perlman

text: I have a method. It's called practice as needed.
author: Itzhak Perlman

text: It takes considerably more art and skill to play the violin lightly than it does to play it loudly. The best possible training is learning to play pianissimo and without pressure.
author: Yehudi Menuhin

text: Ravi represents enlightenment, organization, discipline — and yet such freedom. Freedom that is won through a great deal of thought, work, practice and experience.
author: Yehudi Menuhin

text: When one puts up a building, one makes an elaborate scaffold to get everything into its proper place. But when one takes the scaffold down, the building must stand by itself. That is how a musician should work.
author: Andrés Segovia
source: Widely documented in guitar pedagogy literature

text: The guitar is the easiest instrument to play, and the hardest to play well.
author: Andrés Segovia
source: Widely documented reflection on the guitar

text: The advice I am giving always to all my students is above all to study the music profoundly.
author: Andrés Segovia

text: I have found that if you love life, life will love you back.
author: Arthur Rubinstein
source: Widely documented reflection on his life philosophy

text: Music is not a hobby, not even a passion with me; music is me.
author: Arthur Rubinstein

text: It is not so good, in a musical way, to overpractice. When you do, the music seems to come out of your pocket.
author: Arthur Rubinstein

text: At every concert I leave a lot to the moment. I must have the unexpected, the unforeseen. I want to risk, to dare.
author: Arthur Rubinstein

text: That way the music can bloom anew. It's like making love — the act is always the same, but each time it's different.
author: Arthur Rubinstein

text: The faculty of creating is never given to us all by itself. It always goes hand in hand with the gift of observation.
author: Igor Stravinsky
source: Poetics of Music in the Form of Six Lessons, Harvard Norton Lectures, 1939–40

text: My freedom will be so much the greater and more meaningful the more narrowly I limit my field of action and the more I surround myself with obstacles.
author: Igor Stravinsky
source: Poetics of Music in the Form of Six Lessons

text: Invention presupposes imagination but should not be confused with it.
author: Igor Stravinsky
source: Poetics of Music in the Form of Six Lessons

text: Nothing can possibly take the place of listening to music.
author: Aaron Copland
source: What to Listen for in Music (1939)

text: Contemporary music, especially, is created to wake you up, not put you to sleep. It is meant to stir and excite you, to move you.
author: Aaron Copland
source: What to Listen for in Music (1939)

text: Most people seem to resent the controversial in music; they don't want their listening habits disturbed.
author: Aaron Copland
source: What to Listen for in Music (1939)

text: There is no end of learning.
author: Robert Schumann

text: By means of industry and perseverance you will rise higher and higher.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Without enthusiasm nothing great can be effected in art.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Play always as if in the presence of a master.
author: Robert Schumann

text: It is better to rest than to work without pleasure and vigour.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Endeavour to play easy pieces well and with elegance; that is better than to play difficult pieces badly.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Never jingle! Play always with energy, and do not leave a piece unfinished.
author: Robert Schumann

text: If all would play first violin, we could not obtain an orchestra. Therefore esteem every musician in his place.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Do not be afraid of the words Theory, Thoroughbass, Counterpoint; you will understand their full meaning in due time.
author: Robert Schumann

text: You must be able to understand a piece of music upon paper.
author: Robert Schumann

text: Cultivation of the ear is of the greatest importance. Endeavour early to distinguish each several tone and key.
author: Robert Schumann

text: One must find this technique with the fingers themselves, must feel it out.
author: Vladimir Horowitz

text: Speaking is more difficult than playing.
author: Martha Argerich

text: Do not hurry. This is a fundamental rule. If you hurry and collapse or tumble down, nothing is achieved.
author: Shinichi Suzuki

text: If a musician wants to become a fine artist, he must first become a finer person.
author: Shinichi Suzuki

text: If he or she really goes about it in earnest, anyone can cultivate ability in ten years, I believe.
author: Shinichi Suzuki

text: Teaching music is not my main purpose. I want to make good citizens.
author: Shinichi Suzuki

text: To study music, we must learn the rules. To create music, we must break them.
author: Nadia Boulanger

text: One can never train a child carefully enough. The eyes are trained, but the ears very little.
author: Nadia Boulanger

text: It's an oral art. Until you hear it, it is not music, and if it sounds good, it is good music.
author: Duke Ellington

text: Music is my mistress, and she plays second fiddle to no one.
author: Duke Ellington

text: My favorite tune? The next one. The one I'm writing tonight or tomorrow — the new baby is always the favorite.
author: Duke Ellington

text: How can anyone expect to be understood unless he presents his thoughts with complete honesty?
author: Duke Ellington

text: There are simply two kinds of music, good music and the other kind. The only yardstick by which the result should be judged is how it sounds.
author: Duke Ellington
source: Widely documented reflection, echoing his 1957 interview

text: Playing 'bop' is like playing Scrabble with all the vowels missing.
author: Duke Ellington
source: Interview with Richard O. Boyer, The New Yorker, July 1, 1944

text: Just because you're not a drummer doesn't mean you don't have to keep time.
author: Thelonious Monk
source: Recollected by Steve Lacy, from his time playing with Monk in 1960

text: My music is the spiritual expression of what I am — my faith, my knowledge, my being.
author: John Coltrane

text: I experienced, by the grace of God, a spiritual awakening which was to lead me to a richer, fuller, more productive life.
author: John Coltrane

text: Making the simple complicated is commonplace; making the complicated simple, awesomely simple, that's creativity.
author: Charles Mingus

text: Practicing is a sign of morality in a musician.
author: Wynton Marsalis
source: Moving to Higher Ground: How Jazz Can Change Your Life (2008)

text: Virtuosity is the first sign of morality in a musician. It means you're serious enough to practice.
author: Wynton Marsalis
source: Moving to Higher Ground: How Jazz Can Change Your Life (2008)

text: You've got to respect the gift God gave you by learning your craft.
author: Quincy Jones

text: If you are going to miss, miss quick. As long as you don't repeat your mistakes and make them habits, you are progressing.
author: Quincy Jones

text: Once you have that foundation, you can do anything you want to musically and your music can evolve.
author: Quincy Jones

text: Just don't give up trying to do what you really want to do. Where there is love and inspiration, I don't think you can go wrong.
author: Ella Fitzgerald

text: It isn't where you came from, it's where you're going that counts.
author: Ella Fitzgerald

text: When I sing, I believe. I'm honest. You have to reach out to them with total honesty and humility.
author: Frank Sinatra

text: Throughout my career, if I have done anything, I have paid attention to every note and every word I sing.
author: Frank Sinatra

text: I think it's because I get an audience involved, personally involved in a song — because I'm involved myself.
author: Frank Sinatra

text: Each day I feel that a man my age should try to learn something new, something different.
author: B.B. King

text: Even now, if I don't learn something every day, you know what I think? It's a day lost.
author: B.B. King
source: Widely documented interview reflection, on turning 82

text: I never practice as much as I should. And it's laziness on my part, so I'm nothing like as good as I should be.
author: B.B. King

text: If a fan says, 'Oh, you're wonderful,' I thank them, and I go home and get my guitar and practice.
author: B.B. King

text: What does the world need with just another good musician? The world needs good people.
author: Victor Wooten
source: Recounting advice from his mother, in interviews and The Music Lesson

text: Anyone who practices enough will get the ability. That's easy.
author: Victor Wooten

text: There are very few bands where the bass player is the leader — I don't see how any one person can be the main thing in any group.
author: Jaco Pastorius

text: I did all my learning in the clubs. Playing eight sets a night.
author: Jaco Pastorius

text: One good thing about music: when it hits you, you feel no pain.
author: Bob Marley

text: You had to work at it and learn your craft: how to take care of your voice, how to pace your concerts, all that trial and error.
author: Aretha Franklin
source: Interview reflection on her early career

text: Well, maybe I'm just taking care of my voice.
author: Aretha Franklin

text: My mentor was Clara Ward of the famous Ward gospel singers. My dad was my coach. And my natural love for music is what drove me.
author: Aretha Franklin

text: I don't know what life would be like without music. In my lonely times, music has been my closest friend.
author: Stevie Wonder
source: Quoted in Conversations with Maya Angelou (1989)

text: Every time you pick up your guitar to play, play as if it's the last time.
author: Eric Clapton

text: I came into music during the great folk scare of the early '60s. You could learn the guitar and pretend to be a songwriter — and maybe you turned out to be one.
author: James Taylor

text: My stuff is an internal thing expressed in music, and you hope that things that mean something to you will resonate with other people.
author: James Taylor

text: It would not be a good song, in my eyes, until it went through a period of sensitivity to a moment of clarity.
author: Joni Mitchell

text: I thrive on change. That's probably why my chord changes are weird, because chords depict emotions.
author: Joni Mitchell

text: I'll tell you what freedom is to me: no fear.
author: Nina Simone

text: These are some of the best musicians around, so I practice and practice and practice.
author: Chet Atkins
source: Interview reflection, on performing with the Boston Pops

text: I committed to always being a student and always being open to growth.
author: Beyoncé

text: Vision and intention weren't enough; I had to put in the work.
author: Beyoncé

text: In learning you will teach, and in teaching you will learn.
author: Phil Collins
source: Song lyric, "Son of Man," from Disney's Tarzan (1999)

text: I've finally accepted the fact that singing takes ten minutes to explain and ten years to accomplish.
author: Renée Fleming
source: The Inner Voice: The Making of a Singer (2004)

text: If I go three days without vocalizing, the voice is gone.
author: Luciano Pavarotti

text: If you want to learn music you have to practice music!
author: Luciano Pavarotti

text: With singing, you never know when you are going to lose the voice, and that makes you appreciate the time that you have when you are still singing well.
author: Luciano Pavarotti

text: It's taken me all my life to learn what not to play.
author: Dizzy Gillespie

text: If you feel safe in the area that you're working in, you're not working in the right area. Always go a little further into the water than you feel you're capable of being in.
author: David Bowie

text: All my big mistakes are when I try to second-guess or please an audience. My work is always stronger when I get very selfish about it.
author: David Bowie

text: I love getting on a big writing binge and staying up a couple days working on a song.
author: Dolly Parton

text: It's how I express myself, how I express how I see things.
author: Dolly Parton
